name: Dark Matter Barrage
tips: Your top-end board wipe — at 7 mana it's the most expensive standard cast in the set and eats most of a 10-mana pool, so you're casting it on a turn where you've already built mana and DM stacks over the preceding turns.

The damage scales with your TOTAL Dark Matter (summed across every live friendly minion), not just a single Mage's stack. Only Mage-tribe minions ever bank DM — go wide with Mages (Shadow Blaster, Gargoyle Sorceress, Eclipse Shade, Ratchanter, Grave Caller, Erebus) and feed them DM grants over several turns to stack the pool before casting.

Clean-up spell against token swarms (Common Rats, Diodebots, Rat-conjured boards). Baseline 5 damage halves a 10🤍 token — with 5+ DM banked the barrage one-shots them outright, so build the pool before you pull the trigger.
The +1 DM to every ally Mage means the board you keep after the wipe is stronger than before — this card accelerates your own ramp even when it only partially clears the enemy side.
Against a single large enemy minion, Matter of Time (4 mana, same DM scaling, plus burn) is more mana-efficient. Save Barrage for turns where you're hitting three or more targets.
rulings: Damage is `5 + (sum of Dark Matter stacks across your live minions)`. Enemy DM does not contribute.
Damage resolves before the +1 DM grant, so newly-granted DM does NOT scale this cast's damage — it's banked for the next one.
Every enemy minion on the board takes the full amount — range, tribe, and element are irrelevant.

The +1 DM targets ally [[Mage]]s only — any friendly minion with Mage in its tribe qualifies, including Ratchanter (Mage Rat) and Grave Caller (Mage Undead). Non-Mage allies (Robots, Machines, plain Rats) get nothing.
If you have zero Mages on the board, the second effect is a no-op — the spell still costs 7 mana.
trivia: At 7 mana with board-wipe-scale payoff, Dark Matter Barrage intentionally sits at the apex of the Dark curve — the 'what all this ramp was for' payoff for DM decks.
